title: SiloCut - Zero-Dependency NLE & Screen Recorder
short_description: A standalone Windows non-linear video editor (NLE) and GDI screen recorder written in Rust, packing a timeline editor, multi-track audio mixing, and H.264 MP4 export into a single ~11MB executable with no external runtime dependencies (no FFmpeg, no DLLs).
long_description:
SiloCut is a standalone Windows non-linear video editor (NLE) plus screen recorder built as a single self-contained Rust binary (~11MB) with no external runtime dependencies - no FFmpeg, no bundled DLLs. The GUI is an egui/eframe immediate-mode application with a media bin, preview viewport, and a custom-drawn timeline canvas supporting clip dragging, edge-trimming, playhead scrubbing, snapping, per-track mute/solo/volume, waveforms, and thumbnails. The genuinely non-trivial engineering sits in the media plumbing: a shared codec module configures the OpenH264 encoder with a resolution-scaled bitrate and periodic keyframes, parses the MP4 avcC record into Annex-B SPS/PPS to prime the decoder, and converts length-prefixed NAL units to Annex-B; separate preview, export, and thumbnail subsystems reuse it to demux with symphonia and decode with OpenH264.

The screen recorder runs on its own thread and calls raw Win32 GDI APIs (BitBlt/GetDIBits) to capture the desktop, composites the cursor, converts BGRA to RGB in parallel with rayon, and encodes to H.264/MP4 on the fly with the pure-Rust mp4e muxer - timestamping each frame with real elapsed time so playback speed stays correct even when capture can't sustain the target FPS. A rodio-based real-time mixer queries the timeline every ~100ms and mixes overlapping clips with per-clip fades and per-track volume/solo, and the editor supports razor split, ripple delete, drag-coalescing undo/redo, and .silocut JSON project persistence. Tests cover the codec roundtrip, the parsers (fuzz-tested), and the editing operations, run in Windows CI.
key_features: Single ~11MB standalone Windows binary with zero external runtime dependencies (no FFmpeg/DLLs)
GDI screen recorder with cursor compositing, multi-monitor selection, 30/60 FPS, and pause/resume, encoding to H.264/MP4 on the fly
Wall-clock-accurate recording: frames timestamped with real elapsed time to keep playback speed correct
Custom H.264 plumbing: avcC-to-Annex-B SPS/PPS parsing, decoder re-priming across seeks, and NAL format conversion
Custom-drawn timeline with clip move/trim, snapping, scrubbing, waveforms, and thumbnails
Real-time multi-track audio mixer (rodio) with per-clip fades and per-track volume/solo/mute
Non-destructive edits (razor split, ripple delete) and drag-coalescing undo/redo
Project save/load to a .silocut JSON file and H.264 MP4 export with bitrate scaling

key_challenges.challenge: OpenH264's default encoder settings (low bitrate, frame-skipping, no periodic keyframes) produced an unwatchable, unseekable stream for full-screen capture and export.
key_challenges.solution: A shared encoder builder computes a resolution-and-framerate-scaled bitrate, disables frame-skipping, selects the screen-content usage profile, and forces a keyframe roughly once per second.

key_challenges.challenge: MP4 stores H.264 SPS/PPS parameter sets in the avcC box rather than inline, so the decoder was never given them and produced no frames.
key_challenges.solution: Parsed the avcC box into an Annex-B header used to prime the decoder on open and re-prime it after every seek, and converted samples from length-prefixed to Annex-B format.

title: Clean MD to Multi-Format — VS Code Extension
short_description: A published VS Code extension that converts Markdown to watermark-free PDF, HTML, and Word/DOCX via a markdown-it -> Puppeteer pipeline, with CSS themes, batch conversion, and full editor/Explorer integration.
long_description:
A marketplace-published VS Code extension (TypeScript) that wires the editor's command surface to a three-target document-conversion pipeline. The core PDF path parses Markdown with markdown-it (html/linkify/typographer enabled), injects heading IDs and an optional table of contents, wraps the result in a themed HTML document, and drives a headless Chromium through Puppeteer (page.pdf with configurable A4/Letter/Legal size and margins) to produce clean, watermark-free PDFs. HTML export reuses the same parse/theme step, and DOCX export hand-walks the markdown-it token stream, mapping heading/paragraph/list/fence tokens onto the docx library's document model.

The extension surface is genuinely complete: five registered commands, an Explorer right-click entry scoped to .md files, a language-gated keybinding, a QuickPick format picker, a contributed configuration schema (output folder, page size, TOC toggle, theme), progress notifications, and multi-select batch conversion. Syntax highlighting is provided via highlight.js and several CSS themes are bundled. It is a functional, shipped tool demonstrating solid fluency with the VS Code API and a working multi-format rendering pipeline.
key_features: Markdown-to-PDF via markdown-it rendered through headless Chromium/Puppeteer with configurable page size and margins
Multi-format export - PDF, standalone or linked-CSS HTML, and Word/DOCX - from the same parsed source
DOCX generation by hand-walking markdown-it tokens into the docx library's document model
Five VS Code commands plus an Explorer .md context-menu entry and a language-gated keybinding
QuickPick format picker and multi-select batch conversion with per-file progress notifications
Contributed configuration schema (output folder, page size, TOC toggle, theme) read at runtime
Auto-generated table of contents, heading anchors, and CSS themes with highlight.js syntax highlighting

key_challenges.challenge: Producing watermark-free, print-quality PDFs from Markdown without a paid conversion service.
key_challenges.solution: Rendered themed HTML through headless Chromium via Puppeteer's page.pdf with printBackground, explicit margins, and page-size mapping, waiting for network idle so assets settle before capture.

key_challenges.challenge: Supporting three output formats (PDF, HTML, DOCX) without duplicating the Markdown parsing logic.
key_challenges.solution: Centralized markdown-it parsing, TOC generation, and theme CSS in shared modules reused by the PDF and HTML converters, with a separate token-walking path for DOCX.

title: Color by Numb3r — Full-Stack E-Commerce & Content Platform
short_description: Architected and deployed a multi-tenant e-commerce and content platform for a creative business, featuring a custom React CMS, automated product syncing, and a robust FastAPI backend.
long_description:
Architected and deployed a comprehensive e-commerce and content management platform for a niche creative business. The system features a multi-tenant architecture serving three distinct user groups: Customers (Storefront), Designers (Creative Tools), and Admins (Management).

Key capabilities include:
1. **High-Performance Infrastructure:** Hosted on a custom Linux VPS with Nginx reverse proxy and fully automated CI/CD via GitHub Actions.
2. **Advanced Backend Engineering:** Powered by FastAPI and MongoDB (Beanie), featuring an automated background sync engine that aggregates products from external marketplaces (Bol.com) and a ScrapingAnt service to bypass anti-bot protections.
3. **Modular Frontend:** A complex React 19 SPA with distinct layouts for Public, Admin, and Designer portals, plus a bespoke CMS with rich-text editing and Cloudinary image optimization.
key_features: Multi-Tenant Architecture (Customer, Designer, Admin)
Automated External Product Sync (Bol.com) with ScrapingAnt
Asynchronous Job Queue using FastAPI BackgroundTasks
Custom React CMS with Rich-Text & Cloudinary Integration
Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) & API Key Management
Automated CI/CD Pipeline (GitHub Actions -> VPS)
Performance optimized with Nginx & In-memory Caching

key_challenges.challenge: Synchronizing Data from External Marketplaces
key_challenges.solution: Engineered a robust scraping service using ScrapingAnt and an async job queue to reliably fetch and normalize product data and reviews from Bol.com without blocking the main API.

key_challenges.challenge: Managing Complex User Roles and Content
key_challenges.solution: Built a modular SPA with React Router v7 to handle distinct layouts for different user roles and developed a bespoke CMS to allow admins to manage blogs and layout content easily.

media.caption: Project Demo Video

title: Autonomous Lead Generation AI
short_description: Developed a proprietary, modular Python application designed to autonomously identify, verify, and qualify real estate leads, fully integrated with the Zoho Ecosystem.
long_description:
Developed a proprietary, modular Python application designed to autonomously identify, verify, and qualify real estate leads (Residential, Commercial, Hospitality). The system is deployed on a Virtual Private Server (VPS) and runs on a strict weekly schedule to ensure a continuous pipeline of high-quality leads.

Key capabilities include:
1. **High-Availability Infrastructure:** Hosted on a Linux VPS for performance, executing complex scraping and AI tasks without local resource constraints, with orchestration via Zoho.
2. **5-Stage Data Pipeline:** Engineered robust scrapers (ALN, Crexi, Yelp, Google Maps) with rotation logic, followed by a normalization engine to unify data structures.
3. **AI Verification & Enrichment:** Integrated OpenAI (GPT-5 Nano) to verify facility services from property websites and utilized Apollo.io/RocketReach to retrieve and validate decision-maker contact info.
4. **Scoring & Routing:** Implemented a logic-based scoring engine (0-100) to route high-confidence leads (>75) directly to Zoho CRM via OAuth 2.0.
key_features: Multi-Source Extraction (ALN, Crexi, Yelp, Google Maps)
AI Service Verification using OpenAI GPT-5 Nano
Deep Enrichment via Apollo.io & RocketReach
Logic-based Scoring Engine & Territory Router
Automated Zoho CRM Injection (OAuth 2.0)
Modular CLI Interface for granular control
Deployed on Linux VPS with weekly batch schedules

key_challenges.challenge: Bypassing Sophisticated Bot Detection
key_challenges.solution: Implemented undetected_chromedriver with robust rotation logic and proxy management to scrape data from high-security sources like Google Maps and Yelp without interruption.

key_challenges.challenge: Ensuring Data Quality and Relevance
key_challenges.solution: Integrated OpenAI's GPT-5 Nano with Pydantic structured outputs to analyze unstructured property descriptions, verifying specific amenities (e.g., Valet Parking) to strictly qualify leads before enrichment.

media.caption: Project Demo Video

title: Intelligent Invoice Processing Automation
short_description: Architected and deployed a sophisticated low-code/code-full workflow using n8n to automate financial operations, bridging Gmail and Airtable with Generative AI for invoice parsing.
long_description:
Architected and deployed a sophisticated low-code/code-full workflow using n8n to automate financial operations. The system serves as a bridge between communication channels (Gmail) and database systems (Airtable), utilizing Generative AI to parse unstructured invoice data into structured financial records.

Key capabilities include:
1. **Event-Driven Architecture:** Configured webhooks and triggers within n8n to monitor Gmail in real-time for specific invoice attachments or subject line patterns.
2. **AI Data Extraction:** Integrated Google’s Gemini LLM via API to act as an intelligent parser. The AI agent analyzes PDF/Image invoices, extracting key fields (Invoice ID, Line Items, Tax, Vendor Details) regardless of the document layout, replacing rigid OCR templates.
3. **Cloud Sync & Management:** Automated file handling to archive raw documents into structured Google Drive directories and orchestrated record creation in Airtable for finance team approval.
key_features: Event-Driven Architecture with n8n webhooks
Real-time Gmail monitoring for invoices
AI Data Extraction using Google Gemini LLM
Layout-agnostic parsing (PDF/Image)
Custom JavaScript logic for currency conversion
Automated Google Drive archiving
Seamless Airtable integration for approvals

key_challenges.challenge: Handling Unstructured Invoice Data
key_challenges.solution: Integrated Google Gemini LLM to analyze PDFs and images, extracting structured data without rigid OCR templates.

key_challenges.challenge: Automating Complex Financial Logic
key_challenges.solution: Utilized custom JavaScript nodes in n8n to handle currency conversion, data sanitization, and conditional logic based on totals.

media.caption: Project Demo Video

title: Ask Sunnah - AI Islamic Scholar
short_description: A production-deployed RAG platform (asksunnah.online) that answers Hadith questions in any language from seven authentic collections with cited sources, plus an image/OCR Hadith authenticity verifier - built end-to-end across an async FastAPI backend, a MySQL->OpenAI->Pinecone ingestion pipeline, and a Next.js 16 frontend.
long_description:
Ask Sunnah is a solo-built, three-repository RAG system that answers Hadith questions in natural language, grounded in retrieved excerpts from seven collections (Bukhari, Muslim, Mishkat, Nasa'i, Abu Dawood, Tirmidhi, Ibn Majah), each traceable to a sunnah.com reference. An offline Python pipeline extracts Hadith from a MySQL database, strips HTML, counts tokens with tiktoken, generates 3072-dimension embeddings with OpenAI's text-embedding-3-large, and upserts them into per-collection namespaces of a serverless Pinecone index.

The async FastAPI backend serves a multi-gate RAG pipeline: for non-English queries a single combined LLM call detects the language, translates to English, and classifies the query; the English query is embedded (Redis-cached) and all selected Pinecone namespaces are queried concurrently via asyncio.gather over a thread pool (wrapping Pinecone's synchronous client) so N collection lookups overlap instead of running serially; an answer is generated with gpt-5-nano and translated back into the user's original language and script. Beyond retrieval it is a fairly complete SaaS - email/OTP signup with bcrypt hashing and opaque session tokens, free/basic/pro plus anonymous tiers with Redis-cached per-day/per-month quotas, and IP-keyed rate limiting - plus a second pipeline that verifies the authenticity of an uploaded Hadith image via gpt-5-nano vision with OpenAI structured outputs. The Next.js 16 / React 19 frontend delivers the chat UI, verifier, account flows, an isolated admin dashboard, and production SEO/PWA.
key_features: Multi-gate RAG pipeline: combined language-detect + translate + classify, embedding, concurrent multi-namespace retrieval, generation, and translate-back
Concurrent vector retrieval across Pinecone namespaces (asyncio.gather + to_thread), cutting an N-collection query toward single-query latency
Multilingual: any-language input is answered in the user's own language and script, with citations to sunnah.com
Hadith authenticity verifier: OCR of uploaded images via gpt-5-nano vision + OpenAI structured outputs, then retrieval and a typed verdict with confidence
Subscription tiers and quotas (free/basic/pro + anonymous) enforced with Redis read-through cached counts
Full auth: email/OTP signup (SHA-256-hashed OTPs), bcrypt passwords, opaque session tokens, and an admin panel API
Offline ETL pipeline (MySQL -> tiktoken -> OpenAI embeddings -> Pinecone) with a staged CLI and checkpoints
Next.js 16 frontend with chat, verifier, and dual user/admin auth; Dockerized with GitHub Actions + systemd deploy

key_challenges.challenge: Pinecone's Python client is synchronous, so querying multiple collection namespaces would block the event loop and run serially inside an async FastAPI app.
key_challenges.solution: Wrapped each namespace query in asyncio.to_thread and fanned them out with asyncio.gather, overlapping network round-trips so total latency approaches a single query while preserving per-namespace result ordering.

key_challenges.challenge: Supporting questions in any language while keeping retrieval quality (embeddings are English-indexed) and returning fluent answers in the user's own script.
key_challenges.solution: A combined single-call detect+translate+classify step (with an English fast-path and a two-call fallback on failure), followed by a translate-back stage that preserves the user's writing style, so failures degrade to English rather than erroring.

key_challenges.challenge: Enforcing per-user daily/monthly quotas without hammering the database on every request, and stopping anonymous users from resetting limits by rotating headers.
key_challenges.solution: Redis read-through cached counts with dated quota keys, plus anonymous caps that match on both session id and server-derived client IP so omitting or rotating the session header cannot bypass the limit.

media.caption: Project Demo Video
User interface showing a query and response
Example of AI-generated answer with sources
Selecting specific Hadith collections for search
Mobile responsive view of the chat interface

title: Slack Prayer Reminder Bot
short_description: A production-ready bot that sends beautiful, motivational reminders to Slack channels with Quran verses and AI-generated messages.
long_description:
This is a robust, automated bot designed to enrich a Slack workspace with timely and inspiring prayer reminders. The application runs on a continuous schedule, performing two key jobs:

1.  **Daily Setup:** Each morning, the bot fetches the day's prayer times from the AlAdhan API for a specific location. It then uses the Google Gemini AI to generate a unique, motivational message for each of the five prayers and stores this data in a local SQLite database.

2.  **Reminder Dispatch:** Every minute, the bot checks the database for upcoming prayers. When a prayer is due (e.g., 10 minutes beforehand), it pairs the AI-generated message with a randomly selected Quran verse (in both Arabic and Urdu) and sends a beautifully formatted, multi-section message to the designated Slack channel. This ensures that reminders are not only punctual but also fresh and engaging every day.
key_features: Automated, scheduled prayer reminders for Slack
Unique AI-generated motivational messages via Google Gemini
Integrated Quran verses with Arabic and Urdu text
Richly formatted, visually appealing Slack messages
SQLite persistence to prevent duplicate reminders and ensure reliability
Secure configuration using environment variables for API keys
Configurable for any location and prayer calculation method

key_challenges.challenge: Ensuring Reliability and Punctuality
key_challenges.solution: Architected the bot with a two-part scheduling system. A daily job pre-fetches and stores all necessary data (prayer times, AI messages) in an SQLite database. A separate, frequent job then only checks the local database. This decouples the bot from real-time API dependencies, making it resilient to network failures and ensuring reminders are always sent on time.

key_challenges.challenge: Creating Engaging and Non-Repetitive Content
key_challenges.solution: To avoid message fatigue, I integrated Google's Gemini AI to generate a fresh, context-aware motivational message for each prayer, every single day. This was combined with a system that randomly selects from a large corpus of Quran verses, guaranteeing that the content remains inspiring and varied.

media.caption: Example Fajr prayer reminder in Slack
Isha prayer notification with Quran verse
A motivational message for Maghrib prayer
Overview of the project's file structure

title: SweetTales - Chat to PDF Memory Book
short_description: A web application that transforms WhatsApp chat exports into beautifully themed, printable PDF memory books, with optional AI-powered highlights.
long_description:
SweetTales is a Flask-based web application created to turn digital conversations into tangible keepsakes. The user journey is simple: upload a standard WhatsApp chat export (.txt file), choose from a selection of visual themes, and add a custom title and name. 

The backend then performs a multi-step process: 
1. **Parsing:** It intelligently parses the unstructured text file, identifying senders, timestamps, messages, and filtering out system notifications (e.g., 'user was added'). 
2. **HTML Generation:** The parsed data is dynamically rendered into a styled HTML document using a Jinja2 template corresponding to the user's chosen theme. 
3. **AI Highlights (Optional):** If an API key is provided, the entire chat transcript is sent to the Google Gemini API to generate a summary of key moments, which is added as a special 'Highlights' section in the book. 
4. **PDF Conversion:** The application uses Playwright's headless browser engine to convert the final HTML into a high-fidelity, print-ready PDF, perfectly preserving the theme's styling and layout.
key_features: Parses standard WhatsApp chat export (.txt) files
Multiple customizable themes for PDF styling
High-quality, print-ready PDF generation via Playwright
Optional AI-powered conversation highlights using Google Gemini
Simple and intuitive web interface for upload and customization
Asynchronous PDF generation to prevent request timeouts on large chats
Modular code structure for easy maintenance and theme additions

key_challenges.challenge: Reliably Parsing Unstructured WhatsApp Chat Data
key_challenges.solution: Developed a robust chat parser using regular expressions to accurately handle various message formats, including multi-line messages, different timestamp patterns (12h/24h), and special characters. The parser was designed to effectively filter out non-message system entries to create a clean conversation log.

key_challenges.challenge: Generating High-Fidelity PDFs with Complex CSS
key_challenges.solution: Standard PDF libraries struggled to render modern CSS themes accurately. I chose to integrate Playwright, a headless browser automation tool, to render the chat into an HTML page first and then 'print' it to PDF. This ensures that any theme, no matter how complex its CSS, is rendered with perfect fidelity, exactly as it would appear in a Chrome browser.

media.caption: The main application UI for file upload and customization.
The professional landing page for the SweetTales application.
Example of a generated PDF cover page.
An inside page of the generated PDF showing the chat layout.
Another example of the PDF output with a different theme.

title: AskQuran - AI Quranic Scholar
short_description: A three-tier RAG system that answers questions about the Holy Quran with source-cited verses, pairing a Python ETL pipeline and a stateless Flask API with a Next.js chat UI, powered by Google Gemini and a Pinecone vector database.
long_description:
Ask Quran is a personal, end-to-end RAG application across three repositories implementing the full lifecycle of a semantic-search-plus-generation system. A modular Python ETL pipeline runs through an argparse CLI in three independently-invocable stages (extract, embed, index): it merges per-Surah metadata with verse translations into structured records, generates embeddings with Google's gemini-embedding-001 (batched), and upserts them into a Pinecone 'quran' namespace. Two details stand out - after embedding it truncates each vector to the configured dimension and re-normalizes it to unit L2 length so cosine similarity stays valid, and it uses distinct task types (RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT at index time vs RETRIEVAL_QUERY at query time). The pipeline is idempotent and resumable, skipping completed stages and populated namespaces unless --force flags are passed.

The API is a cleanly structured, stateless Flask service (application-factory pattern, blueprints, services layer, prompt manager, request validator). Its notable design choice is a two-path flow: an LLM classification layer first uses a few-shot Gemini prompt to decide whether a query is conversational or a genuine Quran question, then routes accordingly. Quran queries proceed through the RAG pipeline - embed the query, retrieve top-k verses from Pinecone, format them with references and quran.com links, and feed them into a citation-enforcing system prompt that instructs Gemini (temperature 0.2) to answer strictly from the supplied verses. The Next.js 15 / React 19 frontend renders the answer alongside an accordion of retrieved source verses linking out to Quran.com.
key_features: Query classification layer routing conversational vs. Quran questions via a few-shot Gemini prompt
RAG generation grounded in retrieved verses with a system prompt that forbids outside information and enforces inline citations
Semantic retrieval from Pinecone using Gemini query embeddings, returning verses with references and quran.com deep links
Embedding correctness: vector truncation followed by L2 unit-normalization, with separate index-time vs query-time task types
Modular three-stage ETL pipeline (extract / embed / index) runnable end-to-end or per-stage via a CLI
Idempotent, resumable pipeline that skips completed work unless --force flags are supplied
Stateless Flask API (app factory, blueprints, services layer, validation, /health) deployed with gunicorn
Next.js chat UI with a typed API client and a retrieved-verses accordion

key_challenges.challenge: Distinguishing casual or meta messages (greetings, 'who are you?') from real Quran questions so retrieval and generation aren't wasted on non-substantive input.
key_challenges.solution: Added an LLM classification step with a strict few-shot prompt that returns a JSON object for Quran queries and plain prose for conversational ones, with a JSON-parse failure treated as the conversational branch.

key_challenges.challenge: Keeping cosine similarity meaningful when using truncated Gemini embedding dimensions across both indexing and querying.
key_challenges.solution: Implemented explicit L2 unit-normalization after truncating vectors to the configured dimension on both sides, and used matching RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT vs RETRIEVAL_QUERY task types for indexing and querying.

key_challenges.challenge: Preventing the LLM from hallucinating religious content or citing verses it wasn't given.
key_challenges.solution: Authored a constrained system prompt requiring answers derived exclusively from the retrieved verses, mandating inline citations, and specifying an explicit 'cannot form a complete answer' response when context is insufficient, run at low temperature.

media.caption: Main user interface showing an AI-generated answer with source verses.
The clean and inviting chat interface.
Display of retrieved Quranic verses that support the AI's answer.
Responsive design for mobile devices.

title: LearnXcellence: AI-Powered E-Learning & Proctoring Platform
short_description: A full-stack e-learning platform featuring custom-trained AI models for intelligent proctoring, AI-assisted content generation, and biometric identity verification.
long_description:
LearnXcellence is a sophisticated e-learning platform built from the ground up to address the needs of modern education, with a strong focus on academic integrity. The platform is built on a decoupled, three-tier architecture: a React.js frontend for a dynamic user experience, a Node.js/Express backend for core business logic and API services, and a dedicated Python/Flask microservice for handling all intensive AI/ML operations. 

For instructors, it offers a full suite of course management tools, including AI-powered quiz and project idea generation. The standout feature is the intelligent proctoring system, which analyzes a student's webcam feed in real-time for violations like phone usage, multiple people, and gaze deviation. 

For students, the platform provides a secure and engaging learning environment. A key innovation is the use of a custom-trained AI model for facial liveness detection during registration and quiz initiation, preventing identity spoofing with photos or screens. The system culminates in generating a data-backed 'cheating probability' score, providing instructors with actionable insights and detailed reports.
key_features: Advanced AI Proctoring with custom-trained models (phone, gaze, multi-person detection)
AI-Based Liveness & Anti-Spoof Detection for secure identity verification
AI Content Generation for quizzes, project suggestions, and viva questions
Decoupled Microservice Architecture (React, Node.js, Python/Flask)
Distinct Role-Based Dashboards for Instructors and Students
Comprehensive Course & User Management functionalities
Automated Grading with manual override and detailed PDF reporting
Secure user authentication with facial biometrics and JWT

key_challenges.challenge: Ensuring Robust Academic Integrity in Online Exams
key_challenges.solution: Developed a multi-faceted AI proctoring system. This involved training separate YOLOv8 models for mobile phone and multi-person detection, and integrating MediaPipe for gaze and mouth movement analysis. The data from these analyses was fed into a custom-trained Scikit-learn classifier to generate a nuanced cheating probability score, moving beyond simple rule-based flags.

key_challenges.challenge: Preventing Identity Spoofing during Registration and Exams
key_challenges.solution: Instead of relying on basic face recognition, I trained a custom YOLOv8 model specifically for liveness detection. This model was trained on a proprietary dataset of real faces vs. spoofing attempts (e.g., photos on screens, printed images), allowing the system to reliably verify that a real, live person is in front of the camera.

key_challenges.challenge: Architecting a Scalable System for Intensive AI Tasks
key_challenges.solution: Designed a microservice architecture to separate the main application logic (Node.js) from the computationally heavy AI processing (Python/Flask). This prevents AI tasks like image analysis from blocking the user interface, ensures independent scalability of the services, and allows for using the best technology for each specific job.

media.caption: Instructor's dashboard for managing courses and students.
Detailed AI Proctoring report with cheating probability and violation evidence.
Student's dashboard showing enrolled courses.
The landing page for the LearnXcellence platform.

title: QuoteWise - AI Quote Generation Platform
short_description: A full-stack social platform built with Angular and Node.js where users can generate unique quotes using the Google Gemini AI, share them, and interact with the community.
long_description:
QuoteWise is a complete social web application designed for creative expression, built on a modern, end-to-end TypeScript stack. The core of the platform is an AI-powered quote generator that allows authenticated users to provide a prompt and receive a unique, concise quote generated by the Google Gemini API. 

The application is architected with an Angular SSR frontend for enhanced performance and SEO, and a robust Node.js/Express backend. Beyond generation, it's a full-fledged community platform. Users have comprehensive profiles displaying their created quotes, liked posts, and favorited items. A central feed showcases all community quotes, where users can engage by liking, commenting, and saving quotes to their personal collection. Security is handled with JWT-based authentication, ensuring all user-specific routes and actions are protected.
key_features: AI-powered quote generation via Google Gemini API
End-to-end TypeScript stack (Angular frontend, Node.js backend)
Server-Side Rendering (Angular SSR) for improved SEO and performance
Full user authentication with JWT (signup, login, protected routes)
Interactive social features: Likes, Comments, and Favorites
Comprehensive user profiles to showcase personal activity
Community quote feed for discovering new content

key_challenges.challenge: Building a Type-Safe Full-Stack Application
key_challenges.solution: Leveraged TypeScript across the entire stack—for the Angular frontend and the Node.js/Express backend. This created an end-to-end type-safe environment, significantly reducing runtime errors, improving developer experience, and making the API contract between the client and server more robust and self-documenting.

key_challenges.challenge: Managing Application State and Reactivity
key_challenges.solution: Utilized Angular's built-in services and the RxJS library to manage application state. By using Observables to stream data from the backend to the components, the UI updates reactively and efficiently whenever data changes (e.g., a quote is liked or a new comment is posted), ensuring a smooth and dynamic user experience.

media.caption: The AI quote generation interface where users input a prompt.
The main quote feed where users can see community-generated content.
User profile page displaying created, liked, and favorited quotes.
Viewing a single quote with its comments and interaction options.
The landing and login page for the application.

title: ChefConnect - Recipe Sharing Platform
short_description: A full-stack recipe-sharing platform where users can discover, create, and review culinary creations, built with Next.js and NextAuth.js for a modern, secure experience.
long_description:
ChefConnect is a full-stack web application built using the modern Next.js 13+ App Router, allowing users to create, share, and discover recipes. The platform provides a complete user journey, from registration and login to creating and managing personal content. Authentication is seamlessly handled by NextAuth.js, supporting both traditional email/password credentials and social logins via GitHub.

The application features a RESTful API built directly within the Next.js framework for all CRUD operations on recipes, users, and reviews. Robust authorization middleware is implemented to ensure that users can only edit or delete their own content. The frontend is fully responsive, styled with Tailwind CSS, and utilizes Formik and Yup for robust, client-side form validation, creating a polished and secure user experience.
key_features: Full Recipe CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete)
Secure user authentication with NextAuth.js (Credentials & GitHub)
Interactive review and rating system for recipes
User profiles with personal recipe galleries
Ownership-based authorization (users can only edit their own content)
Built with the modern Next.js 13+ App Router
Fully responsive design styled with Tailwind CSS

key_challenges.challenge: Implementing Secure and Flexible Authentication
key_challenges.solution: Integrated NextAuth.js to handle the complexities of authentication. This provided built-in providers for both GitHub (OAuth) and traditional Credentials, simplifying session management, JWT handling, and security. Next.js Middleware was then used to protect both API routes and client-side pages from unauthorized access.

key_challenges.challenge: Creating a Cohesive Full-Stack Experience in a Single Framework
key_challenges.solution: Leveraged the Next.js App Router to co-locate API routes, server components, and client components. This allowed for creating a self-contained RESTful API for all CRUD operations (recipes, reviews) directly within the project, simplifying the architecture and streamlining development without needing a separate backend server.

media.caption: The main homepage displaying a gallery of recipes.
Detailed recipe view with ingredients, instructions, and author details.
The form for creating and adding a new recipe.
User profile page showcasing their submitted recipes.

title: WaterZilla - Water Delivery Management System
short_description: A full-stack MERN application for managing a water delivery service, featuring a comprehensive admin dashboard and a user-friendly customer portal. Uniquely includes two interchangeable backends (MySQL/Sequelize and MongoDB/Mongoose).
long_description:
WaterZilla is a complete management system designed to digitize and streamline the operations of a water delivery business. The application is architected with a modern React frontend and offers the flexibility of two complete, interchangeable backend implementations: one with Node.js, MySQL, and Sequelize for a relational approach, and another with Node.js, MongoDB, and Mongoose for a NoSQL approach. 

The platform provides two distinct experiences: 
1. **Customer Portal:** A secure and intuitive interface where customers can register, log in, browse products, place orders, view their order history with real-time status tracking, and leave reviews. 
2. **Admin Dashboard:** A powerful, all-in-one control center for business owners. It includes an analytics dashboard with charts, full CRUD management for orders, customers, products, and partner companies, and a reporting engine that can export sales data to PDF. Product images are handled via Cloudinary integration.
key_features: Dual Backend Implementation (MySQL with Sequelize & MongoDB with Mongoose)
Comprehensive Admin Dashboard with real-time analytics and charts
Full CRUD Operations for orders, customers, products, and companies
Secure user and admin authentication using JWT
Customer portal with order placement, history, and status tracking
PDF report generation for sales and order data
Product image uploads and management via Cloudinary

key_challenges.challenge: Demonstrating Database Versatility and Adaptability
key_challenges.solution: Architected and built two complete, independent backend systems. One uses a relational database (MySQL with Sequelize) to enforce strict schema and data integrity, while the other uses a NoSQL database (MongoDB with Mongoose) for flexibility and scalability. This showcases the ability to choose the right database technology based on project requirements.

key_challenges.challenge: Creating a Centralized, Data-Rich Admin Control Panel
key_challenges.solution: Developed a feature-complete single-page application dashboard using React. It consolidates all critical business functions into one interface, leveraging the Recharts library to provide clear data visualizations and the jsPDF library to enable one-click PDF report exporting, offering actionable insights for administrators.

key_challenges.challenge: Managing Global Application State on the Frontend
key_challenges.solution: Utilized React's Context API to manage global state, such as user authentication status and profile information. This provided a clean and efficient way to share data across various components in both the customer portal and the admin dashboard without relying on more complex state management libraries.

media.caption: The comprehensive admin dashboard showing analytics and order management.

title: Whirley Bird - Gesture-Controlled Pygame
short_description: An interactive arcade game built with Pygame where players control the bird's movement in real-time using hand gestures via their webcam and the MediaPipe framework.
long_description:
Whirley Bird is a unique take on the classic arcade genre, replacing traditional keyboard or mouse inputs with an intuitive, gesture-based control system. The game uses a computer's webcam to capture the player's hand movements. This video feed is processed in real-time by OpenCV and Google's MediaPipe framework to accurately track the hand's position. This allows players to intuitively steer the bird left and right on screen simply by moving their hand.

The gameplay is challenging and dynamic: players must dodge falling obstacles while collecting apples to increase their score. The game's speed progressively increases, offering a scalable challenge. To encourage replayability, all high scores are saved locally in an SQLite database, allowing players to compete against their personal best.
key_features: Innovative Gesture-Based Control via webcam
Real-time Hand Tracking with Google MediaPipe
Classic arcade gameplay with progressive difficulty
Persistent High Score tracking using a local SQLite database
Built with Pygame for the game engine and OpenCV for video processing
Complete game loop with welcome, pause, and game-over screens

key_challenges.challenge: Integrating Real-Time Computer Vision with a Game Loop
key_challenges.solution: The primary challenge was bridging the gap between the OpenCV video feed and the Pygame game window. This was solved by capturing the hand's landmark coordinates from MediaPipe, normalizing them, and then scaling them to map accurately to the bird's permissible horizontal movement within the game's canvas, ensuring responsive and intuitive control.

key_challenges.challenge: Maintaining Performance and a High Frame Rate
key_challenges.solution: To ensure a smooth experience, the hand-tracking logic was encapsulated in a separate, efficient module. This module processes the video feed without blocking Pygame's main game loop, allowing both the computer vision analysis and the game rendering to run at a high, stable frame rate.

media.caption: The game in action, showing the bird, obstacles, score, and the OpenCV hand-tracking window.

title: LetterGen - AI Cover Letter & Email Generator
short_description: A modern web app that uses Google Gemini AI to generate personalized cover letters and emails by parsing a user's PDF resume and a job description.
long_description: LetterGen is a productivity tool designed to automate the tedious task of writing unique cover letters. The application allows a user to upload their PDF resume and paste a job description. It then leverages a sophisticated, decoupled three-tier architecture to deliver results. The React frontend sends the data to a Node.js API server, which handles the file upload. The Node server then forwards the request to a dedicated Python/Flask microservice. This AI service parses the text from the PDF using PDFPlumber, combines it with the job description, and uses the Google Gemini API to generate 5 distinct, high-quality variations of a cover letter or application email. For user privacy, uploaded resumes are deleted immediately after processing.
key_features: AI-powered generation of cover letters & emails via Google Gemini
Automatic text extraction from user-uploaded PDF resumes
Generates 5 distinct variations for each request
Decoupled microservice architecture (React, Node.js, Python/Flask)
Privacy-focused: resumes are deleted from the server after processing
Clean, responsive, and intuitive user interface with animations

key_challenges.challenge: Designing a Scalable and Efficient System for AI Tasks
key_challenges.solution: Architected a decoupled microservice system to separate concerns. A Node.js server handles lightweight API requests and file management, while a separate Python/Flask service manages the computationally intensive tasks of PDF parsing and communication with the Gemini AI. This prevents the main API from being blocked by long-running AI jobs and allows for using the best language for each task.

key_challenges.challenge: Securely Handling and Processing User-Uploaded Files
key_challenges.solution: Implemented a secure workflow for handling sensitive documents. The Node.js server uses Multer for in-memory file handling, forwards the file to the Python service for immediate processing, and is designed to delete the temporary file from the server as soon as the AI generation is complete, ensuring user data is not stored.
